pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states '2-minute sampling protocol'; 2 minutes = 120 seconds. Verifier checks duration > 100 seconds (verifier.py line 66), but task description specifies 2-minute minimum.
Task description explicitly states 'Record at least 5 samples' (task.json line 4); verifier checks len(csv_rows) >= 6 (header + 5 data rows) (verifier.py line 91)

Task description explicitly states 'Create a CSV file at '/home/ga/Desktop/monitor_sample.csv' with header 'Time,HeartRate,SpO2'' (task.json line 3); verifier checks for 'time', 'heart'/'hr', 'spo2'/'sat' (verifier.py lines 109-111)

Task requires creating simulated 'Multiparameter Monitor' device adapter using OpenICE; setup_task.sh sources task_utils.sh which calls ensure_openice_running; data is synthetic from simulator, not real patient data
Verifier checks: 40 <= hr <= 160 (verifier.py line 124); confirmed realistic range covering athletes (40 bpm), normal adults (60-100 bpm), and infants (up to 160 bpm)
Verifier checks: 80 <= spo2 <= 100 (verifier.py line 124); clinically normal SpO2 is 95-100%, so 80-94% range includes concerning values but is appropriate for simulator bounds

privileged_info_summary: The task is a longitudinal physiological data sampling exercise using OpenICE medical device simulator (synthetic data). The agent must create a Multiparameter Monitor device adapter, record 5+ samples of Heart Rate and SpO2 values over a sampling period into monitor_sample.csv with header 'Time,HeartRate,SpO2', and write an analysis report to sample_analysis.txt. File paths are /home/ga/Desktop/monitor_sample.csv and /home/ga/Desktop/sample_analysis.txt. Minimum sample count is 5. The task description specifies a 2-minute (120 seconds) sampling protocol, though metadata lists min_duration_sec as 100. Physiological bounds: Heart Rate 40-160 bpm, SpO2 80-100%. The verifier checks device creation, file existence, duration > 100 seconds, data validity, and report accuracy against CSV calculations.
